analysis: Underground structures beneath known Great Circle sites

features.name: Osiris Shaft beneath Giza causeway
features.notes: Partially flooded, 3 levels, sarcophagus at bottom

features.name: Subterranean Chamber of the Great Pyramid
features.notes: Carved from bedrock 30m below base, unfinished

features.name: ScanPyramids void (2017 muon tomography)
features.notes: ~30m long void above Grand Gallery, discovered by muon tomography

features.name: Saqqara Serapeum
features.notes: ~400m of tunnels, 24 massive granite sarcophagi (60-80 tons each)

features.name: Saqqara ibis catacombs
features.notes: ~4 million mummified ibis, km of tunnels

features.name: Saqqara baboon catacombs
features.notes: Adjacent to ibis catacombs

features.name: Djoser Step Pyramid substructure
features.notes: 28m deep shaft, 5.7km of tunnels beneath pyramid

features.name: Valley of the Kings tomb complex
features.notes: 63 tombs, KV5 alone has 121+ chambers (Weeks 1995)

features.name: Valley of the Queens
features.notes: ~80 tombs including Nefertari's tomb

features.name: Osireion at Abydos
features.notes: Megalithic subterranean temple, granite blocks, water table

features.name: Giza Plateau trial passages / tunnels
features.notes: Exploratory rock-cut passages near Great Pyramid

features.name: Abu Rawash pyramid shaft
features.notes: Deep shaft beneath Djedefre's pyramid

features.name: Medium pyramid substructure
features.notes: Descending passage and burial chamber

features.name: Chavín de Huántar underground galleries
features.notes: 3+ levels, acoustic properties, Lanzón monolith chamber

features.name: Nazca puquios (36+ underground aqueducts)
features.notes: Spiral access points, still functioning, UNESCO tentative list

features.name: Cahuachi buried construction phases
features.notes: Multiple building phases buried beneath mound construction

features.name: Nazca Líneas subterranean water management
features.notes: Underground filtration galleries connected to geoglyphs

features.name: Wari underground chambers at Pikillacta
features.notes: Subterranean storage/ritual chambers

features.name: Naqsh-e Rostam rock-cut tombs
features.notes: 4 Achaemenid royal tombs carved into cliff face, Ka'ba-ye Zartosht

features.name: Persepolis drainage tunnel system
features.notes: Extensive underground water management beneath the platform

features.name: Persepolis Treasury foundations
features.notes: Sub-floor storage and foundation chambers

features.name: Buried city beneath Persepolis (magnetometry 2020s)
features.notes: Magnetometry revealed extensive buried structures beyond visible ruins

features.name: Naqsh-e Rajab rock reliefs
features.notes: Sasanian rock-cut reliefs in natural rock face

features.name: Great Bath and waterproofing/drainage system
features.notes: Bitumen-sealed, earliest known public water management structure

features.name: Underground drainage/sewer network
features.notes: Covered brick drains beneath streets, one of earliest urban sewers

features.name: Unexcavated lower waterlogged layers
features.notes: Estimated 3+ meters of stratigraphy never excavated due to water table

features.name: Well network (~700 wells)
features.notes: Brick-lined wells, highest density of any ancient city

features.name: Ana Kai Tangata (painted cave)
features.notes: Sooty tern (manutara) paintings, ceremony of the Birdman cult

features.name: Ana Te Pahu (lava tube complex)
features.notes: Largest lava tube, agricultural use, banana cultivation inside

features.name: Ana O Keke (virgin cave)
features.notes: Ritual seclusion cave on Poike cliff face

features.name: Numerous habitation lava tubes
features.notes: Multiple lava tubes used as shelters across the island

features.name: Ana Heu Neru (meditation cave)
features.notes: Cave at Orongo with petroglyphs

features.name: Plain of Jars (underground contexts)
features.notes: Some jars associated with cave burial practices

features.name: Tam Pa Ling cave (Laos)
features.notes: Oldest modern human fossils in mainland SE Asia (~46-63 ka)

features.name: Tham Lod rock shelter (Thailand)
features.notes: Paleolithic habitation and coffin cave burials
